title: Diplazium tomentellum
taxonomy: Diplazium tomentellum is classified within the family Athyriaceae, a group of ferns commonly known as the lady ferns. It belongs to the genus Diplazium, which comprises over 400 species of ferns, mostly found in tropical and subtropical regions. The family Athyriaceae is part of the larger group Polypodiales, which includes a wide range of ferns. Diplazium tomentellum specifically is categorized as a species of Diplazium, a genus of relatively small to medium-sized ferns. The classification of Diplazium tomentellum reflects its evolutionary relationships with other ferns within the Athyriaceae family.
uses: Diplazium tomentellum has been found to possess various medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant has been used to treat fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions. Its extracts have shown potential in reducing inflammation and oxidative stress. In folk medicine, it is also used to treat skin conditions, such as eczema and acne. Further research is needed to fully understand its medicinal uses and potential therapeutic applications.

The medicinal parts of Diplazium tomentellum include its rhizome and roots, which are used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments.
